heading: Conservation and disposition of estates
label: 2715c Conservation and disposition of estates

children.label: A
children.text: take possession of the personal effects of the decedent within his jurisdiction;

children.label: B
children.text: inventory and appraise the personal effects of the decedent, sign the inventory, and annex thereto a certificate as to the accuracy of the inventory and appraised value of each article;

children.label: C
children.text: when appropriate in the exercise of prudent administration, collect the debts due to the decedent in the officer’s jurisdiction and pay from the estate the obligations owed by the decedent;

children.label: D
children.text: sell or dispose of, as appropriate, in the exercise of prudent administration, all perishable items of property;

children.label: E
children.text: sell, after reasonable public notice and notice to such next of kin as can be ascertained with reasonable diligence, such additional items of property as necessary to provide funds sufficient to pay the decedent’s debts and property taxes in the country of death, funeral expenses, and other expenses incident to the disposition of the estate;

children.label: F
children.text: upon the expiration of the one-year period beginning on the date of death (or after such additional period as may be required for final settlement of the estate), if no claimant shall have appeared, after reasonable public notice and notice to such next of kin as can be ascertained with reasonable diligence, sell or dispose of the residue of the personal estate, except as provided in subparagraph (G), in the same manner as United States Government-owned foreign excess property;

children.label: G
children.text: transmit to the custody of the Secretary of State in Washington, D.C. the proceeds of any sales, together with all financial instruments (including bonds, shares of stock, and notes of indebtedness), jewelry, heirlooms, and other articles of obvious sentimental value, to be held in trust for the legal claimant; and

children.label: H
children.text: in the event that the decedent’s estate includes an interest in real property located within the jurisdiction of the officer and such interest does not devolve by the applicable laws of intestate succession or otherwise, provide for title to the property to be conveyed to the Government of the United States unless the Secretary declines to accept such conveyance.

children.heading: Authority to act as administrator
children.label: 2 Authority to act as administrator
children.text: Subject to paragraphs (3) and (4), a consular officer may act as administrator of an estate in exceptional circumstances if expressly authorized to do so by the Secretary of State.

children.heading: Exceptions
children.label: 3 Exceptions
children.text: The responsibilities described in paragraphs (1) and (2) may not be performed to the extent that the decedent has left or there is otherwise appointed, in the country where the death occurred or where the decedent was domiciled, a legal representative, partner in trade, or trustee appointed to take care of his personal estate. If the decedent’s legal representative shall appear at any time prior to transmission of the estate to the Secretary and demand the proceeds and effects being held by the consular officer, the officer shall deliver them to the representative after having collected any prescribed fee for the services performed under this section.

children.label: A
children.text: authorized by treaty provisions or permitted by the laws or authorities of the country wherein the death occurs, or the decedent is domiciled; or

children.label: B
children.text: permitted by established usage in that country.

children.heading: Statutory construction
children.label: 5 Statutory construction
children.text: Nothing in this section supersedes or otherwise affects the authority of any military commander under title 10 with respect to the person or property of any decedent who died while under a military command or jurisdiction or the authority of the Peace Corps with respect to a Peace Corps volunteer or the volunteer’s property.

children.heading: Disposition of estates by the Secretary of State
children.label: b Disposition of estates by the Secretary of State

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: After receipt of a personal estate pursuant to subsection (a), the Secretary may seek payment of all outstanding debts to the estate as they become due, may receive any balances due on such estate, may endorse all checks, bills of exchange, promissory notes, and other instruments of indebtedness payable to the estate for the benefit thereof, and may take such other action as is reasonably necessary for the conservation of the estate.

children.heading: Disposition as surplus United States property
children.label: B Disposition as surplus United States property
children.text: If, upon the expiration of a period of 5 fiscal years beginning on October 1 after a consular officer takes possession of a personal estate under subsection (a), no legal claimant for such estate has appeared, title to the estate shall be conveyed to the United States, the property in the estate shall be under the custody of the Department of State, and the Secretary shall dispose of the estate in the same manner as surplus United States Government-owned property is disposed or by such means as may be appropriate in light of the nature and value of the property involved. The expenses of sales shall be paid from the estate, and any lawful claim received thereafter shall be payable to the extent of the value of the net proceeds of the estate as a refund from the appropriate Treasury appropriations account.

children.heading: Transfer of proceeds
children.label: C Transfer of proceeds
children.text: The net cash estate after disposition as provided in subparagraph (B) shall be transferred to the miscellaneous receipts account of the Treasury of the United States.

children.heading: Designation as excess property
children.label: A Designation as excess property
children.text: In the event that title to real property is conveyed to the Government of the United States pursuant to subsection (a)(1)(H) and is not required by the Department of State, such property shall be considered foreign excess property under title IV of the Federal Property and Administrative Services Act of 1949.1See References in Text note below.1

children.heading: Treatment as gift
children.label: B Treatment as gift
children.text: In the event that the Department requires such property, the Secretary of State shall treat such property as if it were an unconditional gift accepted on behalf of the Department of State under and .section 2697 of this titlesection 300(a)(3) of this title

children.heading: Losses in connection with the conservation of estates
children.label: c Losses in connection with the conservation of estates
A
children.text: the conservation of which has been undertaken under or subsection (a) of this section; andsection 2715 of this title

children.label: B
children.text: that has been lost, stolen, or destroyed while in the custody of officers or employees of the Department of State.

children.heading: Exclusion of personal liability after provision of compensation
children.label: A Exclusion of personal liability after provision of compensation
children.text: Any such compensation shall be in lieu of personal liability of officers or employees of the Department of State.

children.heading: Liability to the Department
children.label: B Liability to the Department
children.text: An officer or employee of the Department of State may be liable to the Department of State to the extent of any compensation provided under paragraph (1).

children.heading: Determinations of liability
children.label: C Determinations of liability
children.text: The liability of any officer or employee of the Department of State to the Department for any payment made under subsection (a) shall be determined pursuant to the Department’s procedures for determining accountability for United States Government property.

children.heading: Regulations
children.label: d Regulations
children.text: The Secretary of State may prescribe such regulations as may be necessary to carry out this section.
